ben: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Bengali.
bey: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Beli (New Guinea).
bob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aweer.
bobs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of bob
bone: noun. A composite material consisting largely of calcium phosphate and collagen and making up the skeleton of most vertebrates.
bony: adjective. Resembling, having the appearance or consistence of, or relating to bone; osseous.
bore: verb. To inspire boredom in somebody.
born: verb. past participle of bear; given birth to.
bos: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Bosnian.
boy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bodo (Central Africa).
brens: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of bren
bro: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Brokkat.
brose: noun. Oatmeal mixed with boiling water or milk.
brosy: adjective. In rural and farming circles, stout and strong; well-built; well fed with brose.
bye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pouye.
byre: noun. A barn, especially one used for keeping cattle in.
bys: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Burak.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
neb: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Toura.
nob: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian Bokmål.
nobby: adjective. Wealthy or of high social position; of or pertaining to a nob (person of great wealth or social standing).
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
nosy: adjective. Prying, inquisitive or curious in other’s affairs; tending to snoop or meddle.
obe: noun. A particular subdivision of ancient Laconia.
obey: verb. To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
obeys: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of obey
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
orb: noun. A spherical body; a sphere, especially one of the celestial spheres; a sun, planet, or star
orby: adjective. having characteristics of an orb
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
oyer: noun. A hearing in a civil case which is based on the content of a document, in which the plaintiff is required to produce the document.
rob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tae'.
robe: noun. A long loose outer garment, often signifying honorary stature.
robs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rob
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
snob: noun. A person who wishes to be seen as a member of the upper classes or of some other elite (such as the masters of a certain craft) and
snobbery: noun. The property or trait of being a snob.
snore: verb. To breathe during sleep with harsh, snorting noises caused by vibration of the soft palate.
snye: verb. Obsolete spelling of sny (“abound, swarm, teem, be infested”).
sob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sobei.
sobber: noun. One who sobs; a weeper.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
sorb: noun. The service tree, Sorbus domestica.
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
syn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Senaya.
yen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Yendang.
yes: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yeskwa.
yob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yoba.
yon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yongkom.
yores: pronoun. Eye dialect spelling of yours.
